recent_decisions.committee: Tourism, Economy and Communities Scrutiny  Committee
recent_decisions.title: Committee noted Housing and Homelessness Annual Report
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the annual report on housing and homelessness, including concerns about private rented sector viability and rough sleeping figures. They requested updates on selective licensing and SWEP funding.
key_data_points: 9 individuals estimated to sleep rough nightly
8 additional flexible beds provided for SWEP

recent_decisions.committee: Tourism, Economy and Communities Scrutiny  Committee
recent_decisions.title: Committee noted Cultural Strategy progress
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted progress on Blackpool's Cultural Strategy, including partnerships, events, and funding initiatives like the £30,000 Creative Seed Funding programme.
key_data_points: 830 young people engaged in Cultural Passport survey
Monet exhibition confirmed for Grundy Art Gallery
Black Light music festival scheduled for June 2026

recent_decisions.title: Approved minutes of last meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The minutes of the last meeting held on 13 January 2026 were approved and signed by the Chair as a correct record.

recent_decisions.title: Excluded public from meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The public was excluded from the meeting under Section 100(A) of the Local Government Act 1972 due to exempt information.

recent_decisions.title: Granted Private Hire Driver’s Licence to S.P. with severe warning
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee granted a Private Hire Driver’s Licence to S.P. with a severe warning due to past driving offences and a caution.

recent_decisions.title: Deferred consideration of D-R.C.’s application
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee deferred consideration of D-R.C.’s application for a Private Hire Driver’s Licence to the next meeting due to their absence.

recent_decisions.title: Granted Hackney Carriage and Private Hire Driver’s Licence to I.U.L. with severe warning
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee granted a Hackney Carriage and Private Hire Driver’s Licence to I.U.L. with a severe warning due to past drug offences.

recent_decisions.title: Refused Private Hire Driver’s Licence for I.Y.B.
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee refused a Private Hire Driver’s Licence for I.Y.B. due to a historic child sexual abuse offence.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of minutes from previous meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the minutes from the previous meeting held on 20 November 2025 as a true and correct record.

recent_decisions.title: Internal audit follow-up on building repairs and maintenance
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the internal audit follow-up report on building repairs and maintenance, raising concerns about the removal of five-year lifecycle maintenance programmes due to budget constraints.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Insufficient budget to implement five-year lifecycle maintenance programmes
key_data_points: Five-year lifecycle maintenance programmes were removed due to budget constraints
Risk-based condition surveys will inform maintenance prioritisation

recent_decisions.title: Strategic risk register deep dive on strategy
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the strategic risk register deep dive report, which covered risks related to the cost of living crisis, poor health and wellbeing outcomes, lack of quality housing, and the impact of the national waste strategy.
key_data_points: Joint Local Health and Wellbeing Strategy for 2024-2028
Selective Licensing scheme planned over five years
Central area redevelopment with community support

recent_decisions.title: Approval of revised annual governance statement 2024/25
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the revised annual governance statement for 2024/25, which included additional explanations based on external auditor recommendations.

recent_decisions.title: Annual governance statement 2024/25 half-year review
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the half-year review of the annual governance statement and requested that incomplete actions be carried over to the 2025/26 statement with revised deadlines.
key_data_points: Incomplete actions to be carried over to 2025/26
Revised deadlines to be included in future reports

recent_decisions.title: Home to school transport granted for Child SMH on exceptional grounds
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Appeals Committee granted home to school transport for Child SMH for the remainder of the 2025/26 academic year due to exceptional circumstances, despite initial policy rejection.
key_data_points: Transport initially denied due to policy ineligibility
Decision applies for remainder of 2025/26 academic year

recent_decisions.title: Noted financial performance of wholly-owned companies
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the financial performance update of the council's wholly-owned companies as at Month 8 of 2025/26.

recent_decisions.title: Noted Blackpool Transport Services Limited update
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the financial and commercial update presented by Blackpool Transport Services Limited.
key_data_points: Update presented by Managing Director and Finance Director

recent_decisions.title: Approved dissolution of Blackpool Entertainment Company Limited
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee agreed to voluntarily dissolve Blackpool Entertainment Company Limited, transferring its assets and liabilities to Blackpool Tourism Limited.
key_data_points: Assets and liabilities to be transferred to Blackpool Tourism Limited
Maintenance costs highlighted as a concern

motions.text: That Blackpool Entertainment Company Ltd be voluntary dissolved, subject to the completion of relevant transfer agreements.
That all remaining assets, liabilities, contracts, intellectual property and undertakings be transferred to Blackpool Tourism Limited by way of an Asset and Liability Transfer Agreement.
That Councillor Jo Farrell, Anne-Marie Morgan and Cllr Carl Mitchell be authorised to enter into and execute an Asset and Liability Transfer Agreement.
That Blackpool Entertainment Company Limited shall submit the required DS01 (Striking Off Application).

recent_decisions.title: Approval of planning application 25/0586 for 5 apartments at 6-8 Carlin Gate
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the planning application for 5 apartments, despite neighbour objections. The decision was unanimous with no recorded votes.
key_data_points: 47 new enforcement cases registered in October 2025
394 live complaints outstanding as of October 2025
10 parking spaces included in the development

motions.text: The Committee was asked to approve the application subject to the conditions outlined in the Committee Report.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of minutes from the meeting held on 11 November 2025
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously approved the minutes from the previous meeting as a correct record.
key_data_points: No declarations of interest were made
Minutes were approved as a correct record

motions.text: That the minutes of the meeting held on 11 November 2025 be agreed as a correct record.

recent_decisions.title: Noting the Planning Enforcement Update for October 2025
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the enforcement update, which showed 47 new cases and 394 live complaints.
key_data_points: 47 new enforcement cases registered in October 2025
394 live complaints outstanding as of October 2025

motions.text: To note the Planning Enforcement Update.

recent_decisions.title: Council approves 2026/27 Council Tax Reduction Scheme
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Council Tax Reduction Scheme for 2026/27 was approved, maintaining the same reduction for working-age claimants as 2025/26. The motion was carried with no recorded vote.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Maintains existing reduction levels for working-age claimants

motions.text: To note the impact of the Scheme to date. To agree the Council Tax Reduction Scheme 2026/27. To agree that the reduction applied to working age claimants remains the same as the 2025/26 Scheme agreed by Council on 16 December 2024 and that the main elements and method of calculating awards will be the same. To agree to continue to operate a Discretionary Discount Policy to be awarded in cases of exceptional hardship.

recent_decisions.title: Council reappoints committees and adjusts political balance
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Council reappointed its committees with adjusted political balance following recent by-elections. The motion was carried with no recorded vote.

motions.text: To re-appoint the following Council committees, with powers and duties as set out in the Council’s Constitution: Audit Committee, Appeals Committee, Planning Committee, Standards Committee, Adult Social Care and Health Scrutiny Committee, Children and Young People’s Scrutiny Committee, Climate Change and the Environment Scrutiny Committee, Tourism, Economy and Communities Scrutiny Committee, Chief Officers’ Employment Committee. To re-appoint a Scrutiny Leadership Board, with the powers set out

recent_decisions.title: Approved £11m budget savings with 4.99% Council Tax increase
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council approved £11m budget savings (from £12.5m target) with a 4.99% Council Tax increase (2.99% + 2% Adult Social Care precept).
recent_decisions.financial_context: Remaining budget savings after £1.5m income approval
key_data_points: 4.99% Council Tax increase (2.99% + 2% Adult Social Care precept)

motions.text: The Council to agree the proposed remaining recommendations including a Council Tax increase of 4.99% made up of 2.99% plus 2% Adult Social Care precept.

recent_decisions.title: Approved minutes of last meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously approved the minutes of the last meeting held on 3 December 2024.

recent_decisions.title: Approved Private Hire Driver Licence for G.I. with severe warning
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee granted a licence to G.I. despite a 2019 drink-driving conviction, as it was an isolated incident. Vote: unanimous approval.
key_data_points: Licence granted as an exception to policy

motions.text: That G.I. be granted a Private Hire Driver’s Licence subject to the issuing of a severe warning letter indicating that in the event of further issues that the licence may be revoked or suspended.

recent_decisions.title: Formed Public Protection Sub-Committee
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee agreed to form a politically balanced Public Protection Sub-Committee with 7 members, chaired by Councillor P Brookes and vice-chaired by Councillor D Scott.

recent_decisions.title: Approved Private Hire Driver Licence for M.M. with severe warning
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee granted a licence to M.M. despite two motoring convictions, as the offences were not deemed 'multiple' under policy. Vote: unanimous approval.
key_data_points: M.M. had 9 penalty points on licence
One conviction not declared on application
Licence granted as an exception to policy

motions.text: That M.M. be granted a Private Hire Driver’s Licence with a severe warning letter indicating that in the event of further issues that the licence may be revoked or suspended.

recent_decisions.title: Approved new Pavement Café licensing policy
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved a new Pavement Café licensing policy, setting fees at £500 for new applications and £350 for renewals, to be implemented from 9 June 2025 for ten years.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Fee for new Pavement Café licence applications

recent_decisions.title: Rejected Private Hire Driver Licence for J.M. due to driving concerns
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee rejected J.M.'s licence renewal after two Section 59 warnings for dangerous driving in six months. Vote: unanimous rejection.
key_data_points: J.M. received two Section 59 warnings in six months
Vehicle seized by police in December 2025
No response from J.M. to invitation to meeting
Licence not renewed due to driving concerns

motions.text: That the application for renewal of J.M.'s Private Hire Driver Licence be refused due to concerns over their recent driving history.

recent_decisions.title: Noted transfer of delegated powers
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the transfer of delegated powers from the Trading Standards and Licensing Manager to the Commercial Enforcement Manager post.

recent_decisions.title: Approved minutes of last meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the minutes of the last meeting held on 2 December 2025 as a correct record.

motions.text: That the minutes of the last meeting held on 2 December 2025 be approved and signed by the Chair as a correct record.

recent_decisions.title: Noted Licensing Service update
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the Licensing Service update, which detailed 20 new Premises Licence applications, with 12 granted administratively, 1 withdrawn, 1 refused, and 1 granted at a hearing.

recent_decisions.title: Excluded public from meeting for exempt information
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee excluded the public and press from the meeting under Section 100(A) of the Local Government Act 1972 to discuss exempt information.
key_data_points: Public excluded under Section 100(A) of the Local Government Act 1972
Exempt information under Schedule 12A of the Act

motions.text: That under Section 100 (A) of the Local Government Act 1972, the public be excluded from the meeting during consideration of the whole item, including the decisions referred to at Agenda items 3 and 4 on the grounds that it would involve the likely disclosure of exempt information as defined in paragraph 1 of Part 1 of Schedule 12A of the Act.

recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the presentation on the Licensing Service’s process for assessing street collection applications and the current policy.

recent_decisions.title: Annual Report of Shareholder Committee noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Audit Committee noted the Annual Report of the Shareholder Committee for 2024/25, which outlined risk management practices for wholly owned companies.
key_data_points: Shareholder Committee met twice with each WOC
All but one annual audit report considered
Blackpool Entertainment Company has a vacancy for Chair of Audit Committee

motions.text: To note the Annual Report of the Shareholder Committee.

recent_decisions.title: Reputational risk management reviewed
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Audit Committee reviewed reputational risks, including poor performance in Adult Services and climate change mitigation, and agreed to include misinformation online as part of the periodic review of the Council's Strategic Risk Register.
key_data_points: Council communications team monitors social media for misinformation
Adult Services risk of government intervention noted
Net zero by 2030 target deemed challenging

motions.text: Misinformation online negatively impacting the Council’s reputation should be considered as part of the periodic review of the Council’s SRR.

recent_decisions.title: Committee to receive further update on 10-Year Health Plan in 12 months
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee agreed to receive a further update on the 10-Year Health Plan in approximately 12 months' time, following concerns about cost savings and service impacts.
key_data_points: 47% cost reduction target for ICB by December 2025
